name: BioShock® 2
description: Set approximately 10 years after the events of the original BioShock, the halls of Rapture once again echo with sins of the past. Along the Atlantic coastline, a monster has been snatching little girls and bringing them back to the undersea city of Rapture.
requirements:
Minimum:
  • OS *: Windows XP, Vista, Windows 7
  • Processor: AMD Athlon 64 Processor 3800+ 2.4Ghz or better, Intel Pentium 4 530 3.0Ghz Processor or better
  • Memory: 2GB
  • Graphics: NVIDIA 7800GT 256MB graphics card or better, ATI Radeon X1900 256MB graphics card or better
  • DirectX®: DirectX 9.0c
  • Hard Drive: 11GB
  • Sound: 100% DirectX 9.0C compliant sound card or onboard sound
comment1: Crashes non stop. The original version does not work with modern computers. It crashes every 30 seconds to two minutes. Spent hours researching ways to counter this. Even with all the fixes I could find, I only improved this to get maybe five minutes or so before it crashes. Constant saves. Constant crashes. Constant reloads. A shame really, because it looks like it would be a good game otherwise
comment2: This game constantly crashes, won't load, freezes for no reason, and I've had a couple of cases of it just flat out shutting itself off.  Play the remaster instead.  This is unplayable.
comment3: Bioshock 2 is not a bad game, and sometimes It might even be great. So why isn't it great all the time? Well you see the story just isn't anything special and it ultimately leads the gameplay to be very repetitive with certain mechanics I both love and hate at the same time. If you've played the game you know what I'm talking about! It's absolutely still worth playing if you like the first Bioshock, just don't have your expectations too high :)  7/10
